claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
circumstances: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To get started, could you please tell me: **How many children do you have that you'd like me to assess for Child Benefit eligibility?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'd like to know if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have one child I'm asking about - my daughter Alex, she's 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's great to know about Alex, who is 8 years old.

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to understand your living situation better.

**Do you live with Alex, or does she live with someone else?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex doesn't live with me - she lives with her ex-partner.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. Since Alex lives with someone else, I need to ask you a few more questions to determine your eligibility.

**Are you currently making any financial contributions towards Alex's upkeep (such as food, clothes, pocket money, or contributions towards her accommodation)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not currently contributing financially to Alex's upkeep.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know that.

Since Alex lives with someone else and you're not making financial contributions towards her upkeep, I need to understand the timeline better.

**How long has it been since Alex went to live with her ex-partner?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: It's been quite a while now - about a year or so since she went to live with her mum.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information.

Based on what you've told me, I now have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

According to the Child Benefit rules, when a child lives with someone else, you can normally get Child Benefit for 8 weeks after the child goes to live with that person - but only if nobody else is claiming for them. However, you can continue to receive payments for longer than 8 weeks if you're making financial contributions towards the child's upkeep (at least the same amount as the Child Benefit payment).

Since Alex has been living with her mum for about a year and you're not making any financial contributions towards her upkeep, the 8-week period would have long since expired. Additionally, without making contributions, you wouldn't be able to extend your eligibility beyond that initial 8-week period.

Let me now provide you with my eligibility determination:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Result: You are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

The reason is that Alex has been living with her mother for about a year, which is well beyond the standard 8-week eligibility period for children living with someone else. To maintain eligibility beyond those initial 8 weeks, you would need to be making regular financial contributions towards her upkeep (such as food, clothes, or pocket money) at least equal to the Child Benefit payment amount. Since you're not currently making such contributions, you don't qualify.

If your circumstances change in the future and you start making regular financial contributions towards Alex's care, you could potentially become eligible again. In that case, I'd recommend contacting the Child Benefit Office to discuss your situation.

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old and does not live with the claimant. According to the Child Benefit rules for when a child lives with someone else, the claimant can normally receive Child Benefit for 8 weeks after the child goes to live with another person (if nobody else claims). However, Alex has been living with her mother for approximately one year, which is well beyond the 8-week period. Additionally, the claimant is not making any financial contributions towards Alex's upkeep. To continue receiving Child Benefit beyond 8 weeks, the claimant would need to contribute at least the equivalent of the Child Benefit payment amount towards Alex's food, clothes, pocket money, or accommodation. Since neither condition is met, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one daughter, Alex (age 8), who has been living with her mother for approximately one year. The claimant is not making any financial contributions towards Alex's upkeep. Under Child Benefit rules for children living with someone else, eligibility normally extends for 8 weeks unless the claimant makes ongoing contributions. Since this 8-week period has long expired and no contributions are being made, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

The claimant is not making any financial contributions towards Alex's upkeep. Under Child Benefit rules for children living with someone else, eligibility normally extends for 8 weeks unless the claimant makes ongoing contributions. Since this 8-week period has long expired and no contributions are being made, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
